response:
# Triage plan — top 10 findings (ordered by agent_risk)

Note: `crimes verdict --format json` output wasn't included, so this ranks by `agent_risk` (per the PRD's "aggregate risk, not severity alone" rule). The top 10 cleanly map to four of the five categories — no top-10 finding has churn > 0, so the **git-history** bucket is empty here.

## Structural

| # | Finding | agent_risk | Response | Why |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | `crime_00001` God Function `generateInvoice` (`src/billing.ts:50-253`) | 0.63 | **Fix now** | High sev + 0.95 conf. Unblocks safe edits to billing; extracting helpers also makes #9 trivial. |
| 3 | `crime_00005` Temporal Recklessness (`src/date.ts:4-22`) | 0.61 | **Fix now** | Small module, 5 call sites — cheapest place to introduce a `now()` abstraction that #9 can reuse. |
| 9 | `crime_00004` Temporal Recklessness (`src/billing.ts`, 7 sites) | 0.53 | **Defer** | Land with #1 — clock injection naturally falls out of the `generateInvoice` extraction. |
| 10 | `crime_00008` False Identity `calculateRefundPreview` | 0.52 | **Fix now** | Rename-or-split is a minutes-long change with outsized clarity payoff; pair with #11 (logic-in-comments on same function). |

## IA (information architecture)

| # | Finding | agent_risk | Response | Why |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| 2 | `crime_00002` Missing Agent Context | 0.61 | **Fix now** | Cheapest high-leverage fix in the report. An AGENTS.md/CLAUDE.md compounds across every future agent edit. |
| 4 | `crime_00007` String Sprinkles `"enterprise"` (4 files) | 0.60 | **Fix now** | Centralise before a 5th copy lands; touches files in #5 and #6 so do it before they shift. |
| 5 | `crime_00009` Concept Alias Drift (tenant/account/org/team/workspace) | 0.58 | **Defer** | Real cost is a naming decision, not a refactor. Park until product picks the canonical term; don't let an agent guess. |

## Frontend

| # | Finding | agent_risk | Response | Why |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| 6 | `crime_00012` Duplicated Navigation Source (`/settings/billing`) | 0.57 | **Fix now** | Bundle with #8 — same destination, same PR, single nav-canonicalisation. |
| 8 | `crime_00014` Route Metadata Drift (`/settings/billing`) | 0.56 | **Fix now** | Same destination as #6; aligning route/title/nav labels is one diff, not two. |

## Duplication

The duplication category in the top 10 is covered by the IA findings above — `String Sprinkles` (#4) and `Duplicated Navigation Source` (#6) are the duplication signals; the dedicated `duplicate_block` / `near_duplicate_function` detectors didn't fire on this fixture.

## (Slot 7) — judgement call

| # | Finding | agent_risk | Response | Why |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| 7 | `crime_00013` Unfinished Business (`src/todo.ts`, 424/1k LOC) | 0.57 | **Ignore** | The file is named `todo.ts` and exists to demo this detector — treat as fixture, suppress in `.crimes/ignore`. If this were prod code: **fix now**. |

---

## Recommended PR sequencing

1. **PR A — agent context** (#2): drop in `AGENTS.md`. Minutes.
2. **PR B — billing cleanup** (#1 + #4 + #8 + #10 + #11): split `generateInvoice`, inject clock, rename `calculateRefundPreview`, encode the comment rule. One coherent refactor of `src/billing.ts`.
3. **PR C — clock module** (#3): land `src/date.ts` clock abstraction first if PR B will consume it; otherwise fold in.
4. **PR D — enterprise constant** (#4 / `crime_00007`): one-line constant, four import sites.
5. **PR E — billing route alignment** (#6 + #8): collapse nav sources, align route metadata for `/settings/billing`.
6. **Backlog**: #5 (alias drift — needs product call), `crime_00010` God File (resolves naturally after PR B), orphaned routes (`crime_00021-23` — need product call: in-flight or dead?).
